prompt: I write a GIX investor update once a quarter. It's chaotic — I draft something, Greg reviews, we go back and forth on numbers, I pull figures from the financial model, send it out. The quality varies a lot run to run. I want to spec this as a process so next time a Claude Code agent can do most of it without me starting from scratch. Walk me through designing it.
assertions: Spec file written to the expected output path
verify_spec.py on the spec exits 0 (Gate 6 passes)
verify_spec.py --final on the spec exits 0 (all 17 final blocking assertions pass)
Frontmatter has status: verified (Phase 7 promotion completed)
Mermaid block has at least 5 step nodes plus at least one explicit GATE decision node
Procedure includes an explicit review/iteration loop (revisions go back to review)
Requirement Owners table names a real reviewer for the review step (e.g. Greg)
Inputs include the financial model as a named, controllable input
Output metric is testable — names a measurable property of the sent update, not 'output is correct'
Edge Cases section has at least 3 real (non-placeholder) rows
Build handoff file exists at outputs/build-handoff.md and references the spec path
Build handoff names Claude-Code-specific capture mechanisms (hooks, MCP servers)
Session notes describe what happened in each of the 8 phases
